title: Ornithidium affine
intro: Ornithidium affine is a species of flowering orchid, native to Central and South America, characterized by its showy white flowers and slender stems. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ornithidium affine include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. In herbal medicine, Ornithidium affine is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, often combined with other plants, such as echinacea and licorice root, to enhance its immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Ornithidium affine belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae. It is further classified as a genus within the tribe Maxillarieae. The genus Ornithidium itself is part of the subtribe Maxillariinae. Ornithidium affine is native to Central and South America.
